steps.title: Look for growing divergence
steps.content: The hypothesis says females prefer the sound made by males of their own subspecies, and that this preference will drive further genetic and anatomical divergence over time. Direct support should show the sound difference between the subspecies increasing across generations.

steps.title: Reject weaker evidence
steps.content:
• Choice A explains a current wing-shape difference related to flight distance, not divergence over time.
  • Choice C concerns communication messages, not female preference causing subspecies divergence.
  • Choice D says breeding habits stayed the same, which does not support further divergence.
The remaining choice shows the pitch difference widening over several generations. The answer is B.
